seoTitle: Restoration of Antiques and Fine Furnishings | CARSI
metaDescription: Free course on restoring water-affected antiques and fine furniture: material risks, safe handling, gentle cleaning, restorative methods and authenticity.
imageAlt: Australian furniture conservator carefully cleaning a water-damaged antique timber cabinet in a workshop

og.title: Restoration of Antiques and Fine Furnishings | CARSI
og.description: A free introductory course on restoring antique and delicate furniture affected by moisture: bonding and fungal risks, safe handling, gentle cleaning, restorative techniques and protecting provenance.

faq.q: How does moisture damage antique furniture?
faq.a: Prolonged moisture can erode adhesives in aged glued joints, cause warping through dimensional change, encourage fungal colonisation in concealed corners, and trigger chemical degradation of finishes, metals and dyes.

faq.q: Why avoid harsh solvents on antiques?
faq.a: Concentrated solvents such as acetone can damage shellac and original finishes; gentler options like terpene microemulsions and plant-based poultices help lift soiling while preserving patina and authenticity.

Records such as invoices, shipping inventories, maker markings and photographic timelines substantiate ownership and authenticity, helping protect market value and defend against title or valuation disputes.

faq.q: Do I need restoration experience for this course?
faq.a: No. It is an introductory course covering material integrity risks, handling precautions, cleaning technique selection, restorative methodologies, and authentication and appraisal fundamentals.
